content.problemStatement:
There is a tree with NNN vertices, rooted at vertex 111. Vertex iii has the value AiA_iAi​ written on it.

Alice walks along this tree, starting at the root.

When she is at vertex uuu:


If uuu has no children, she stops.
Otherwise, suppose uuu has ccc children. She picks one of them at random (each one has a 1c\frac{1}{c}c1​ probability of being picked), and then moves to it.
Alice also has a score, defined as follows:


Let the vertices she visited be u1,u2,…,uku_1, u_2, \ldots, u_ku1​,u2​,…,uk​
Then, she will forget **exactly one** of these kkk vertices; and her score will be the bitwise xor of the remaining ones.

That is, if she chooses to forget vertex uiu_iui​, then her score is Au1⊕Au2⊕…⊕Aui−1⊕Aui+1⊕…⊕AukA_{u_1} \oplus A_{u_2} \oplus \ldots \oplus A_{u_{i-1}} \oplus A_{u_{i+1}} \oplus \ldots \oplus A_{u_{k}}Au1​​⊕Au2​​⊕…⊕Aui−1​​⊕Aui+1​​⊕…⊕Auk​​. Here, ⊕\oplus⊕ denotes the bitwise xor operation.


Alice wants to maximize her score, and will always choose to forget a vertex optimally to achieve this.

What is Alice's expected final score?

Find the expected value modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.

That is, the expected value can be written as PQ\frac{P}{Q}QP​ for two integers P,QP, QP,Q such that gcd⁡(Q,109+7)=1\gcd(Q, 10^9 + 7) = 1gcd(Q,109+7)=1; print the value of P⋅Q−1(mod109+7)P\cdot Q^{-1} \pmod{10^9 + 7}P⋅Q−1(mod109+7).


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN — the number of vertices of the tree.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
The next N−1N-1N−1 lines describe the edges. The ithi^{th}ith of these lines contains two space-separated integers uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​, denoting an edge between uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line Alice's expected final score.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 5⋅1055\cdot 10^55⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: There's only one final vertex Alice can reach, taking the path 1→2→31 \to 2 \to 31→2→3. She will choose to forget one of the 333's, for a final score of 3⊕1=23\oplus 1 = 23⊕1=2.
Test case 333: There are three leaf nodes, and Alice has a 13\frac{1}{3}31​ chance of reaching each one.

If she takes the path 1→21 \to 21→2, her score is 222 (she will forget A1=1A_1 = 1A1​=1)
If she takes the path 1→21 \to 21→2, her score is 333 (she will forget A1=1A_1 = 1A1​=1)
If she takes the path 1→21 \to 21→2, her score is 444 (she will forget A1=1A_1 = 1A1​=1)

Her expected score is thus 13⋅(2+3+4)=3\frac{1}{3} \cdot (2 + 3 + 4) = 331​⋅(2+3+4)=3.
